phase.name: 1: Hook Layer — Add keepPreviousData and isFetchingMore

task.name: Update useExpenseTransactionsQuery with placeholderData
task.action: In `src/hooks/useExpenseTransactionsQuery.ts`: 1. Add `keepPreviousData` to the existing import from `@tanstack/react-query`. 2. Add `placeholderData: keepPreviousData` to the `useQuery` options object (after `staleTime`). 3. Destructure `isPlaceholderData` and `isFetching` from the `useQuery` return value (alongside `data`, `isPending`, `error`). 4. In the hook's return object, add: `isFetchingMore: isFetching && isPlaceholderData,`
task.verify: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to confirm no type errors. Run `npx vitest run src/hooks/useExpenseTransactionsQuery.test.ts` to verify existing tests still pass.

task.name: Update useIncomeTransactionsQuery with placeholderData
task.action: In `src/hooks/useIncomeTransactionsQuery.ts`: 1. Add `keepPreviousData` to the existing import from `@tanstack/react-query`. 2. Add `placeholderData: keepPreviousData` to the `useQuery` options object. 3. Destructure `isPlaceholderData` and `isFetching` from the `useQuery` return value. 4. In the hook's return object, add: `isFetchingMore: isFetching && isPlaceholderData,`
task.verify: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to confirm no type errors. Run `npx vitest run src/hooks/useIncomeTransactionsQuery.test.ts` to verify existing tests still pass.

task.name: Update useTransferTransactionsQuery with placeholderData
task.action: In `src/hooks/useTransferTransactionsQuery.ts`: 1. Add `keepPreviousData` to the existing import from `@tanstack/react-query`. 2. Add `placeholderData: keepPreviousData` to the `useQuery` options object. 3. Destructure `isPlaceholderData` and `isFetching` from the `useQuery` return value. 4. In the hook's return object, add: `isFetchingMore: isFetching && isPlaceholderData,`
task.verify: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to confirm no type errors. Run `npx vitest run src/hooks/useTransferTransactionsQuery.test.ts` to verify existing tests still pass.

task.name: Create SkeletonExpenseCard
task.action: Create `src/components/shared/SkeletonExpenseCard.tsx`: - Import `Skeleton` from `@/components/ui/skeleton`. - Use `money-map-card` container class with `flex flex-col gap-3`. - Row 1: icon placeholder (`h-9 w-9 rounded-lg bg-secondary-500`) + name bar (`h-5 w-[160px] bg-secondary-500`) in a `flex items-center gap-2` row. - Row 2: category bar (`h-3 w-[120px] bg-secondary-500`). - Row 3: `flex items-end justify-between` with date bar (`h-3 w-[100px]`) on left and amount bar (`h-5 w-[80px]`) on right. - Export as named export `SkeletonExpenseCard`.
task.verify: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to confirm the component compiles. Visually inspect that the pattern matches existing skeleton components like `SkeletonIncomeTypeCard.tsx`.

task.name: Create SkeletonIncomeCard
task.action: Create `src/components/shared/SkeletonIncomeCard.tsx`: - Same structure as SkeletonExpenseCard but matching IncomeCard layout. - Import `Skeleton` from `@/components/ui/skeleton`. - Use `money-map-card` container class with `flex flex-col gap-3`. - Row 1: icon placeholder (`h-9 w-9 rounded-lg bg-secondary-500`) + name bar (`h-5 w-[160px] bg-secondary-500`). - Row 2: income type bar (`h-3 w-[100px] bg-secondary-500`). - Row 3: `flex items-end justify-between` with date bar (`h-3 w-[100px]`) on left and amount bar (`h-5 w-[80px]`) on right. - Export as named export `SkeletonIncomeCard`.
task.verify: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to confirm the component compiles.

task.name: Create SkeletonTransferCard
task.action: Create `src/components/shared/SkeletonTransferCard.tsx`: - Import `Skeleton` from `@/components/ui/skeleton`. - Use `money-map-card` container class with `flex flex-col gap-3`. - Outer layout: `flex flex-col gap-1` top section + right-aligned amount section. - Row 1: icon placeholder (`h-9 w-9 rounded-lg bg-secondary-500`) + name bar (`h-5 w-[160px] bg-secondary-500`) in `flex items-center gap-2`. - Row 2: transfer type bar (`h-3 w-[100px] bg-secondary-500`). - Row 3: date bar (`h-3 w-[100px] bg-secondary-500`). - Right-aligned section: amount bar (`h-5 w-[80px]`) + accounts bar (`h-3 w-[120px]`) in `flex flex-col items-end`. - Export as named export `SkeletonTransferCard`.
task.verify: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to confirm the component compiles.

task.name: Create SkeletonCompactTransactionCard
task.action: Create `src/components/transactions/SkeletonCompactTransactionCard.tsx`: - Import `Skeleton` from `@/components/ui/skeleton`. - Container: `bg-card border border-border rounded-lg p-3` (matches CompactTransactionCard). - Outer: `flex items-center gap-3`. - Left: icon placeholder `h-8 w-8 rounded-md bg-secondary-500 flex-shrink-0`. - Right (`flex-1 min-w-0`): - Top row (`flex items-start justify-between gap-2`): name bar `h-4 w-[100px]` + amount bar `h-4 w-[60px]`. - Bottom row (`flex items-center justify-between mt-1`): category bar `h-3 w-[80px]` + date bar `h-3 w-[40px]`. - Export as named export `SkeletonCompactTransactionCard`.
task.verify: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to confirm the component compiles.

phase.name: 3: Page Components — Wire up skeleton loading states

task.name: Update Expenses page loading states
task.action: In `src/app/expenses/page.tsx`: 1. Import `SkeletonExpenseCard` from `@/components/shared/SkeletonExpenseCard`. 2. Destructure `isFetchingMore` from `useExpenseTransactionsQuery` (alongside existing destructured values). 3. Replace the mobile card loading state (currently `{isLoading ? (...Loader2 spinner...)` around line 204) with skeleton cards: render 4 `SkeletonExpenseCard` components in a `div` with `grid grid-cols-1 gap-4`. 4. After the `expenseTransactions.map(...)` block and before the Load More button, add a conditional block: `{isFetchingMore && (` render 3 `SkeletonExpenseCard` in `grid grid-cols-1 gap-4 mt-4` `)}`. 5. Update the Load More button condition from `{hasMore && (` to `{hasMore && !isFetchingMore && (`. 6. Keep the desktop table skeleton (`SkeletonTable`) as-is — do not change.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npx tsc --noEmit`. Manually verify in the browser: initial load shows skeleton cards, Load More shows skeleton cards below existing items, items do not flash.

task.name: Update Income page loading states
task.action: In `src/app/income/page.tsx`: 1. Import `SkeletonIncomeCard` from `@/components/shared/SkeletonIncomeCard`. 2. Destructure `isFetchingMore` from `useIncomeTransactionsQuery`. 3. Replace the mobile card loading state (the `isLoading` ternary that shows `SkeletonIncomeTypeCard` cards for the transaction list section) with 4 `SkeletonIncomeCard` components. Note: SkeletonIncomeTypeCard may still be used elsewhere on this page for the income types/budgets section — only replace the transaction list loading skeleton. 4. After `incomeTransactions.map(...)`, add: `{isFetchingMore && (` 3 `SkeletonIncomeCard` in `grid grid-cols-1 gap-4 mt-4` `)}`. 5. Update Load More button: `{hasMore && !isFetchingMore && (`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npx tsc --noEmit`. Manually verify in the browser.

task.name: Update Transfers page loading states
task.action: In `src/app/transfers/page.tsx`: 1. Import `SkeletonTransferCard` from `@/components/shared/SkeletonTransferCard`. 2. Destructure `isFetchingMore` from `useTransfersQuery`. 3. Replace the mobile card loading state with 4 `SkeletonTransferCard` components. 4. After `transfers.map(...)`, add: `{isFetchingMore && (` 3 `SkeletonTransferCard` in `grid grid-cols-1 gap-4 mt-4` `)}`. 5. Update Load More button: `{hasMore && !isFetchingMore && (`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npx tsc --noEmit`. Manually verify in the browser.

task.name: Update TransactionsMobileView loading states
task.action: In `src/components/transactions/TransactionsMobileView.tsx`: 1. Import `SkeletonCompactTransactionCard` from `@/components/transactions/SkeletonCompactTransactionCard`. 2. Destructure `isFetchingMore: expensesFetchingMore` from `useExpenseTransactionsQuery`. 3. Destructure `isFetchingMore: incomeFetchingMore` from `useIncomeTransactionsQuery`. 4. Destructure `isFetchingMore: transfersFetchingMore` from `useTransfersQuery`. 5. For each of the 3 tabs (expenses, income, transfers): a. Replace the `Loader2` spinner loading state with 4 `SkeletonCompactTransactionCard` in a `space-y-2` container. b. After the `map(...)` block, add: `{[tabName]FetchingMore && (` 3 `SkeletonCompactTransactionCard` in `space-y-2 mt-2` `)}`. c. Update Load More button: `{[tabName]HasMore && ![tabName]FetchingMore && (`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npx tsc --noEmit`. Manually verify all 3 tabs in the Transactions page mobile view.

phase.name: 4: Tests — Update hook tests for new return values

task.name: Update useExpenseTransactionsQuery tests
task.action: In `src/hooks/useExpenseTransactionsQuery.test.ts`: 1. Add a test that verifies the hook returns `isFetchingMore` as `false` during initial load. 2. Verify the hook still returns all existing properties correctly (no regressions). No need to test the actual `keepPreviousData` behavior in unit tests — that is TanStack Query's responsibility. Just verify the shape of the returned object.
task.verify: Run `npx vitest run src/hooks/useExpenseTransactionsQuery.test.ts` — all tests pass.

task.name: Update useIncomeTransactionsQuery tests
task.action: In `src/hooks/useIncomeTransactionsQuery.test.ts`: 1. Add a test that verifies the hook returns `isFetchingMore` as `false` during initial load. 2. Verify existing tests still pass.
task.verify: Run `npx vitest run src/hooks/useIncomeTransactionsQuery.test.ts` — all tests pass.

task.name: Update useTransferTransactionsQuery tests
task.action: In `src/hooks/useTransferTransactionsQuery.test.ts`: 1. Add a test that verifies the hook returns `isFetchingMore` as `false` during initial load. 2. Verify existing tests still pass.
task.verify: Run `npx vitest run src/hooks/useTransferTransactionsQuery.test.ts` — all tests pass.

task.name: Lint, build, and full test suite
task.action: 1. Run `npm run lint` — zero errors. 2. Run `npm run build` — zero errors. 3. Run `npx vitest run` — all tests pass. 4. Generate `docs/load-more-skeleton-verification.md` documenting results.
task.verify: All commands exit with code 0. Verification doc is created.
